vulnerabilities.description: CVE-2022-0839 is an XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability in Liquibase Core versions prior to 4.8.0 via the SAXParser. An attacker could exploit this to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery (SSRF) through crafted XML changelog files.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase Secure 4.8.0+ includes the fix. All current Liquibase Secure distributions are built from source with the patched SAXParser configuration.
vulnerabilities.workaround: If using Liquibase < 4.8.0, disable XML changelog parsing or upgrade to 4.8.0+. Alternatively, ensure all changelog files come from trusted sources only.

ratings.justification: Code path containing the vulnerability is not present in Liquibase Secure builds. NVD score reflects theoretical maximum; contextual score reflects actual exploitability.

analysis.detail: XXE vulnerability fixed in Liquibase 4.8.0. Our liquibase-core is built from source with the fix included. Scanner incorrectly matches the non-semantic version format (master-<sha>).
False CPE match. liquibase-commercial-mongodb is a Liquibase extension, not MongoDB server. The vulnerability applies to MongoDB server only.

GraalVM Python setuptools path traversal vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process package inputs; no package installation or processing occurs at runtime.
False CPE match. liquibase-license-utility is not liquibase-core. The vulnerability only affects liquibase-core versions < 4.8.0.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.10.0. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.12.2. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.
Fixed in msal4j 1.15.1. Liquibase Secure uses msal4j 1.23.1, which includes the fix.
False CPE match. cassandra-driver-kerberos is not the Authenticator 2FA app. The vulnerability applies to the Authenticator mobile application.

False positive due to classifier confusion. Trivy reads the JAR's internal pom.properties which reports version 13.2.1 (without the jre11 classifier), causing a false version comparison. The installed JAR is 13.2.1.jre11 which contains the fix. Microsoft's non-standard Maven versioning uses a classifier suffix instead of a standard version bump.

GraalVM Python setuptools ReDoS vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to parse HTML during package lookup; no package lookups occur since all packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin.

GraalVM Python setuptools command injection vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process URLs via download/install functions; those functions are never called at runtime.
